title: Smoky Caramelized Malaysian Shrimp Curry
description: A savory, lowcarb onepot curry that balances sweet, smoky paprika with the bright tang of lime and tamarind. Perfect for a quick dinner that feels gourmet.
instructions: Rinse the shrimp and pat dry with paper towels.
Heat the coconut oil in a medium‑sized pot over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the chopped onion and sauté until translucent, about 3 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic and ginger, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Add the sweet smoked paprika and curry powder, stirring constantly for 1 minute to toast the spices.
Pour in the coconut milk, fish sauce, and tamarind paste; bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, letting the flavors meld for 2 minutes.
Add the shrimp to the pot and cook for 3‑4 minutes, or until they turn pink and opaque, ensuring an internal temperature of at least 140°F (60°C).
Stir in the lime juice, taste, and adjust seasoning with salt or extra fish sauce if needed.
Remove from heat and let rest for 1 minute to allow the flavors to settle.
Sprinkle chopped cilantro over the curry before serving.
